seo.page_title: 2021 Tesla Model 3 import from Japan to NZ
seo.meta_description: Import a 2021 Tesla Model 3 from Japan to NZ with focus on battery health, real-world range, and total landed cost.
seo.h1_tag: Import a 2021 Tesla Model 3 to NZ
seo.og_title: 2021 Tesla Model 3 | Import from Japan to NZ
seo.og_description: Thinking about a 2021 Tesla Model 3? We source from Japan with battery health reports, NZ range guidance and clear landed cost breakdowns.

blocks.hero_h1: Import a 2021 Tesla Model 3 to NZ
blocks.hero_subtitle: Bring a 2021 Tesla Model 3 in from Japan with NZ use in mind. We look at battery health, real-world range and total landed cost before you decide to bid.
proof_points: Transparent pricing — compliance is part of the process
blocks.why_kiwis_buy: The 2021 Tesla Model 3 Long Range offers up to about 570 km on a charge once converted from overseas figures, which works well for NZ weekend trips and daily commuting. Regenerative braking suits stop‑start city traffic and hilly suburbs, while all-wheel drive helps on wet roads. The large touchscreen and app integration make it easy enough to slot into everyday use.

savings_block.headline: Typical savings vs NZ dealer pricing
savings_block.body: Recent 2021 Tesla Model 3s out of Japan usually land in NZ between about $60,000 and $80,000, depending on mileage, grade and options. That often undercuts local dealer pricing for similar cars, even after you factor in shipping, NZ entry certification and on-road costs. We map out the full landed cost before you decide whether a specific car stacks up for you.

cta_form.headline: Find your Model 3 — we'll do the searching
cta_form.subtext: Fill in your details to get started. We'll search the Japanese auctions and come back with real options and pricing by email.

blocks.video_caption: Here’s a 2021 Tesla Model 3 walk‑through, showing the minimalist dash layout, large touchscreen and how the rear seats fold to open up the boot space.
blocks.main_usp_deep: For NZ buyers, the 2021 Tesla Model 3 Long Range stands out for its mix of usable range and tech. The quoted 353 miles translates to roughly 570 km, and we talk through what that looks like in local conditions and seasons. Dual motors and all-wheel drive help with grip on wet or loose sections, while safety tech like forward collision warning and lane keeping assist supports longer drives. We include a battery health report with every car we source so you can see how it’s tracking before you bid.

buyer_persona.headline: Is this car right for you?
buyer_persona.body: You’re likely a tech‑minded professional who wants an EV that can handle the daily commute plus regular trips between cities without constant charging stops. You care about how the battery has been treated, what real NZ range looks like, and how the numbers stack up against a late‑model non-EV sedan. For that mix of logic and tech interest, a 2021 Model 3 imported from Japan is a solid match.

trust_company.headline: Why import with ImportYourCar.co.nz?
trust_company.body: Since 2009 we’ve helped Kiwis bring in cars direct from Japan, including a growing number of EVs like the Tesla Model 3. We give you full auction documentation, translated and explained, along with our view on condition, battery health indicators and likely landed cost. You deal with a real NZ team that talks you through the trade‑offs, not just the upsides. The aim is a clear, step‑by‑step process from first enquiry through to the car arriving here.

car_specs_benefits.spec: Approx. 570 km quoted range (Long Range model, converted from 353 miles)
car_specs_benefits.benefit: Gives enough headroom for most NZ commutes plus weekend trips without needing to charge every day.

car_specs_benefits.spec: 0–100 km/h in around 5.1 seconds (converted from 0–60 mph)
car_specs_benefits.benefit: Plenty of response for motorway on‑ramps and overtakes, even with a full car.
Extra traction on wet roads and loose chip, handy for winter driving and open‑road trips.
Works as a family car or daily commuter, with room for adults in the back.

car_specs_benefits.spec: About 425L of cargo space (converted from 15.0 cu.ft)
car_specs_benefits.benefit: Enough boot space for luggage, groceries or sports gear, plus extra flexibility with folding rear seats.

models_comparison.intro: Here’s how different 2021 Tesla Model 3 variants and options tend to play out when importing from Japan.

rows.notes: For a 2021 Tesla Model 3 you’re well inside current NZ entry rules, including ESC and safety requirements. Later build months may have small software or trim tweaks, which we can confirm from the auction sheet and VIN.

Japan auctions list Standard Range and Long Range variants, with different quoted ranges and performance. Long Range suits regular intercity trips, while Standard Range can work if most driving is local and you want to keep landed cost down.

Spec differences usually show up in wheel design, interior trim colour, audio options and driver‑assist packages. We highlight which features each car has based on photos and auction notes so you’re not guessing.

A 2021 Model 3 typically meets NZ entry standards if documentation is clean and no modifications are noted. As an EV it’s subject to current CO2 rules but avoids running cost‑burn penalties. We still allow for entry certification costs and any remedial work in the landed estimate.

Tesla Model 3s appear regularly in major Japanese auctions, but clean, low‑km Long Range cars can attract strong bidding. If you’re flexible on colour or trim, it can shorten the wait and help with price.

steps.title: Tell us what you're after
steps.body: Fill in the form or message us. We confirm your brief and give you a cost breakdown before anything moves forward.

steps.title: We search the auctions
steps.body: We monitor USS, TAA, HAA and others. For EVs like the Model 3, we look closely at battery‑related notes, mileage and use pattern on each shortlisted car.

steps.title: You approve, we bid
steps.body: We send you the auction sheet and our recommendation. Once you're happy, you pay a deposit and we bid on your behalf.

steps.title: Shipping to NZ
steps.body: Typically 2–4 weeks depending on the sailing schedule. For less common variants or colours, allow a little extra time once the auction clears. We keep you updated along the way.

steps.title: Compliance and delivery
steps.body: The car goes through NZ entry certification and compliance. Once it's through, we arrange delivery or you can collect.

risks_solutions.intro: Importing a 2021 Tesla Model 3 is straightforward, but it helps to understand the main moving parts.

items.risk: You’re unsure how much the battery has degraded and what that means for future range.
items.solution: We focus on cars with clear auction documentation and service history, and provide a battery health summary based on the available data. If documentation is thin or something doesn’t add up, we’ll recommend skipping that car.

items.risk: Worried about hidden costs between the auction hammer price and getting the car on your driveway.
items.solution: We break the landed cost into auction price, shipping, insurance, NZ entry certification, port fees and our fee. You see the full estimate up front, plus where there’s room for variation, before you decide to bid.

items.risk: Not sure how to read auction grades and what they mean for a used EV.
items.solution: We explain the grade, panel notes and interior comments in plain language, and point out anything that might affect value or future use. You get the original auction sheet and our take on it, not just a summary line.

blocks.how_to_use_iyc: You tell us the sort of 2021 Tesla Model 3 you’re after — range, budget, mileage and any must‑have features. We scan the Japanese auctions, filter out the weak options and send you a shortlist with photos, auction sheets and landed cost estimates. If one looks right, we handle the bidding, shipping and NZ side; if not, we keep looking until something sensible comes up.

faq_block.q: What real‑world range can I expect from a 2021 Tesla Model 3 in NZ?
faq_block.a: The quoted 353 miles for the Long Range model converts to about 570 km, but real‑world NZ range is usually lower. How and where you drive, temperature and use of heating or air con all play a part, so we talk through what’s realistic for your driving pattern.

faq_block.q: How much does it cost to import a 2021 Tesla Model 3 from Japan?
faq_block.a: Most 2021 Model 3s we see land between about $60,000 and $80,000, depending on variant, mileage and condition. The total includes the auction price, shipping and insurance, NZ entry certification, port charges, our fee and on‑road costs like registration and plates.

faq_block.q: Will a 2021 Tesla Model 3 from Japan meet NZ compliance standards?
faq_block.a: A 2021 Model 3 typically meets NZ entry standards if documentation is clean and no modifications are noted, but final sign‑off only happens at NZ entry certification. We avoid cars with obvious red flags in the auction notes to improve the odds of a smooth process.

faq_block.q: How long does it take from winning an auction to driving the car in NZ?
faq_block.a: Once the car is bought, shipping to NZ usually takes 2–4 weeks depending on the sailing schedule, then you need time for border checks, entry certification and registration. In practice, most buyers see 6–10 weeks from auction win to driving, with some variation.

faq_block.q: What sort of condition can I expect from a used 2021 Tesla Model 3 at auction?
faq_block.a: Auction grades give a good guide to exterior and interior condition, but they’re still a snapshot. We review the sheet, photos and notes, and call out things like scratches, wheel rash or interior wear so you know what you’re buying before we bid.

subscription_block.headline: Stay in the loop
subscription_block.subtext: Get updates on Tesla Model 3 auction listings, recent landed prices and new EV import guides for NZ.

blocks.company_positioning: ImportYourCar.co.nz is an NZ-based import service that’s been sourcing vehicles from Japan since 2009. We handle a mix of EVs, hybrids and conventional cars, focusing on clear auction information and realistic landed cost estimates. Our role is to help you decide if importing a specific car makes sense for how you drive and what you want to spend.

landed_cost_breakdown.intro: Your landed cost is the total to get a 2021 Tesla Model 3 from a Japanese auction to registered on NZ roads.

rows.notes: The hammer price in Japan, plus local auction and handling fees. This moves with demand, mileage, grade and spec.
Roll‑on roll‑off shipping from Japan to NZ, marine insurance, and port handling at both ends.
Biosecurity checks, entry certification, any work needed to meet NZ standards, registration and plates.

auction_checklist.why: Gives clues about how the battery has been used and whether the car has done lots of short trips or long runs.
Shows the overall condition and any repairs or paintwork that might affect long‑term value.
Heavy wear on seats or the touchscreen can hint at harder use than the odometer suggests.
Helps spot kerb damage or scrapes that might not be obvious from the main exterior shots.
Confirms whether the car has the driver‑assist and comfort features you’re expecting before you bid.

not_for_you_block.headline: Who this may not suit
items: You want a very low upfront price and don’t mind an older EV with shorter range — a 2021 Model 3 is still a premium option.
You’re uncomfortable with any variation in landed cost and would rather pay a fixed sticker price at a local yard.
You rarely drive beyond a short city commute and don’t need the extra range or performance of a Long Range variant.

alternatives_block.intro: If a 2021 Tesla Model 3 doesn’t quite line up with your budget or use case, there are other EVs worth a look.

rows.better_if: Most of your driving is short‑to‑medium city trips and you want a lower landed cost.
rows.tradeoff: Shorter range than a Model 3 and less performance, but simpler and often cheaper to buy.

rows.better_if: You prefer a compact SUV body style with good range and higher seating position.
rows.tradeoff: Different driving feel and interior layout compared with the Model 3, and pricing can be similar for newer examples.

rows.better_if: You want more space and don’t mind an older platform to keep costs similar.
rows.tradeoff: Larger car and older tech in some areas, with range and battery health varying more between individual cars.

battery_health_block.headline: Battery health on imported 2021 Model 3s
battery_health_block.body: Battery condition is central to whether a 2021 Tesla Model 3 makes sense for you. While we can’t run our own diagnostics until the car is in NZ, we use auction data, mileage and service records to build a picture of how each car has been used. You get that summary before you decide to bid, along with our view on how it’s likely to affect real‑world range over the next few years.
checklist: Favour cars with moderate mileage and consistent use rather than very high or very low km readings.
Look for regular service history entries in the auction documentation where available.
Avoid listings with unclear notes around high‑voltage components or warning lights.
Consider how much range you actually need day to day, so minor degradation is put in context.

charging_block.headline: Charging a 2021 Tesla Model 3 in NZ
charging_block.home_charging: Most owners charge at home overnight using a dedicated wall unit or upgraded socket. That’s usually the cheapest and simplest way to run a Model 3, and for many households it covers nearly all weekly driving. We can talk through what sort of home setup you might need and what to ask your electrician.
charging_block.public_charging: Public fast chargers across NZ make longer trips manageable, especially with the range of the Long Range model. You’ll still want to plan stops on longer routes, but for city‑to‑city travel it’s now straightforward to top up along the way.
caveats: Apartment or shared parking can make home charging trickier, so think about where the car will usually be parked.
Fast charging is handy but relying on it for most charging can add wear over time compared with mainly home charging.
Public charger availability can vary by region, so we suggest checking your regular routes on a charging map before committing.
